visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Height ranges from less than 1 meter to 10 meters
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Successive rows with equal height and spacing within a row

domain_specific_visual.WAVE_DRIVEN_FORMATION: Formed by waves colliding with ice sheet irregularities
domain_specific_visual.ERUPTION_VISIBILITY: Eruptions can reach over 10 meters high

secondary_functions: Serve as hunting platforms for snowy owls

domain_specific_functional.TEMPORARY_FORMATION: Frequently destroyed by storms and warm weather

relationships.description: Waves provide the energy needed for the formation of ice volcanoes.
Commonly observed along the southern coast of Lake Erie.
Commonly observed along the southern coast of Lake Ontario.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Primarily in the Great Lakes region
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Natural phenomenon attracting visitors and impacting local ecology
